summary: All 8 taint flows from automated analysis are false positives. No confirmed exploitable vulnerabilities found.

false_positives.reasoning: $_SERVER['REQUEST_TIME'] is a PHP-set integer, not user-controllable. The value is also wrapped in $this->DB->quote(serialize(...)). No RCE vector exists. The exec() call is on a PDO/SQLite object, not shell exec.

$_FILES['content']['tmp_name'] is set by PHP's upload handler to a server-controlled temp path. The user cannot control this value to achieve path traversal. This is standard PHP file upload handling. Additionally, the ZohoOffice editor requires ELFINDER_ZOHO_OFFICE_APIKEY to be defined and the save() method is only reachable through the elFinder connector which requires admin nonce verification.

The tainted value from get_option() is used only in a strict equality comparison: 'maximize' === $minimize_banner ? 'style="display: block;"' : 'style="display: none;"'. The actual echoed output is always one of two hardcoded literal strings, never the tainted variable itself. No XSS possible.
Same pattern as xss-001. The echoed output is always a hardcoded literal string selected by strict comparison, never the tainted variable. No XSS possible.
Same pattern. Output is always 'true' or 'false' literal strings based on strict comparison. The tainted value is never echoed.
Same pattern. Output is always '' or 'fma__minimized' literal strings based on strict comparison. The tainted value is never echoed.

This is PHPUnit library code bundled inside the svg-sanitizer dependency (application/svg-sanitizer/includes/phpunit/). PHPUnit is a test framework that is never invoked during normal WordPress plugin operation. The DefaultPhpProcess class is not instantiated by any plugin code path. Dead code in production context.

The AJAX handler has proper security: (1) nonce verification with wp_verify_nonce(), (2) capability check requiring manage_options (admin-only), (3) the base URL is hardcoded to https://connect.postmansmtp.com/, (4) the user-supplied 'status' is sanitized with sanitize_text_field() which strips newlines/tags. The attacker can only influence a query parameter value on a fixed trusted domain. This is not exploitable as SSRF - the host cannot be redirected. At most it allows an admin to send arbitrary status strings to the postmansmtp API, which is the intended functionality.

notes: The plugin's main attack surface is the elFinder file manager connector, accessible via wp_ajax_fma_load_fma_ui. This requires authentication (WordPress login) and nonce verification (wp_verify_nonce with 'fmaskey'). The connector grants full filesystem access to admins, which is intentional functionality. Non-admin users have restrictions on PHP file uploads via the acceptedName callback. The REST API routes for banner hide/minimize also require manage_options capability. No unauthenticated entry points were identified.
